title: Audit Log Completeness and Gap Detection

compliance.assurance_rationale: Gap detection on audit stream volume helps ensure a continuous, analyzable set of management-plane audit events exists for review and investigation.
compliance.controlObjective: Supports the intent of NIST 800-53 AU-3 (content of audit records) by confirming an uninterrupted stream of audit events suitable for follow-on analysis, not a single static record layout.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Alert on `UC-5.13.50` with incident ticket and collector health check proving restoration when gaps are detected; retain Splunk run history.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Statistical review of log volume is a lightweight audit/operational review that can trigger deeper audit record inspection when the stream breaks baseline.
compliance.controlObjective: Contributes to audit review, analysis, and reporting by automatically flagging anomalous drops that warrant manual AU-6 follow-up of Catalyst Center admin logs.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Monthly summary of `UC-5.13.50` true-positive gaps with root cause (TA outage, API error, or index routing) and corrective action in the GRC or ITSM system.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:audit:logs`. from the `audit_logs` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/data/api/v1/event/event-series/audit-log`. Default poll interval: 300s (5m). Volume: Variable; ~10-100 events/day in typical environments. RBAC: SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `auditUserName` (string), `auditRequestType` (string), `auditDescription` (string), `auditParentId` (string), `createdDateTime` (epoch milliseconds), `isSystemEvent` (boolean). CIM data model: Change.
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" | bin _time span=1h | stats count as log_count by _time | eventstats avg(log_count) as avg_logs stdev(log_count) as stdev_logs | where log_count < (avg_logs - 2*stdev_logs) OR log_count=0 | eval gap_severity=if(log_count=0, "Complete gap", "Unusual drop") | sort _time
description: Detects gaps or unusual drops in audit log volume that may indicate logging failure, tampering, or collection issues.
value: Gaps in audit logs undermine accountability and compliance. Detecting them ensures the audit trail is continuous and tamper-evident.
implementation: Enable the `audit_logs` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA pointing to `index=catalyst`. The TA polls audit log data from the Catalyst Center Intent API `/dna/intent/api/v1/audit/logs` every 5 minutes. Key fields: `auditRequestType`, `auditDescription`, `auditUserName`, `auditTimestamp`, `auditIpAddress`.
visualization: Time series of `log_count` with overlaid control limits, table of anomalous buckets with `gap_severity`, alert when Complete gap or persistent unusual drop.

references.title: Catalyst Center Audit Log API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Holidays and change freezes producing genuinely quiet audit periods.** During organizational holidays or change freeze periods, no administrative activity occurs on Catalyst Center, which may trigger gap detection. Distinguish by checking whether the gap corresponds to a known holiday or change freeze period. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_holidays` lookup and excluding holiday periods from the gap detection baseline.

**Catalyst Center platform maintenance or restart causing an audit gap.** When Catalyst Center undergoes platform maintenance (upgrade, backup, restart), the audit log stops recording during the downtime. Distinguish by checking whether the gap coincides with platform maintenance visible in `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs" isSystemEvent=true` for pre/post-maintenance entries. Suppress by annotating audit gaps with maintenance window records.

**TA poll failure or Splunk forwarder issue causing data ingestion gap.** The audit log gap may be caused by a failure in the Splunk data collection pipeline rather than an actual audit gap on Catalyst Center. Distinguish by checking `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ExecProcessor "TA_cisco_catalyst"` for collection errors during the gap period. Suppress by monitoring the TA health separately and attributing gaps to collection failures rather than audit completeness issues.

**Low-activity period falling below the minimum event threshold.** Overnight or weekend periods with minimal administrative activity may produce event counts below the gap detection threshold without indicating a true gap. Distinguish by comparing the event count with the historical baseline for the same time window (same day-of-week, same hour). Suppress by using time-of-day-aware baselines rather than a flat threshold.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **audit_logs** input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:audit:logs`.
• At least 14 days of historical audit log data to establish reliable hourly baselines.
• Understand the audit log poll interval — gaps shorter than the poll interval are expected and not indicative of data loss.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Audit log completeness monitoring checks for unexpected gaps in the audit log data stream. This is a meta-monitoring use case that validates the integrity of the audit log collection pipeline.

Key monitoring dimensions:
• **Hourly event count:** Each hour should have a minimum number of audit entries (even idle periods produce system events). The baseline varies by environment.
• **Sourcetype freshness:** The `_time` of the latest `cisco:dnac:audit:logs` event should be within the poll interval + buffer.
• **Sequence gaps:** If audit entries have sequential IDs, gaps in the sequence indicate missed entries.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as hourly_count by _time
| eventstats avg(hourly_count) as baseline_avg stdev(hourly_count) as baseline_stdev
| eval expected_min=max(baseline_avg - 2*baseline_stdev, 1)
| eval gap_status=case(hourly_count=0, "Complete Gap", hourly_count < expected_min, "Below Baseline", 1==1, "Normal")
| where gap_status!="Normal"
| eval hours_ago=round((now()-_time)/3600,1)
| sort _time
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`bin _time span=1h`**: Groups audit events into hourly buckets. Audit log activity varies significantly by hour — business hours are busier than nights/weekends.
• **`eventstats avg(hourly_count) as baseline_avg`**: Computes the average hourly event count across the search window. This baseline is not time-of-day-aware — for more accurate detection, use `| eventstats avg(hourly_count) as baseline by date_hour, date_wday`.
• **`expected_min=max(baseline_avg - 2*baseline_stdev, 1)`**: Sets the minimum expected count at 2 standard deviations below the mean, with a floor of 1 event/hour.
• **`gap_status`**: Classifies each hour as "Complete Gap" (zero events, likely a collection failure), "Below Baseline" (fewer events than expected), or "Normal."

Step 3 — Validate
• **Intentional gap test:** Temporarily disable the `audit_logs` input in the TA, wait 2 hours, re-enable. Verify the gap detection identifies the missing hours within the next poll cycle.
• **Baseline accuracy:** Run `| stats avg(hourly_count) by date_hour, date_wday` to see the expected volume per hour per day-of-week. Night hours (00:00-06:00) and weekends should have lower baselines.
• **Historical completeness:** Compare the total audit entry count in Splunk over 7 days with the count in Catalyst Center > System > Audit Logs for the same period.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** Hourly event count timechart with upper/lower baseline bands highlighted. Gap hours marked in red. Single-value tile: "Hours Since Last Audit Entry."
• **Alert:** Trigger when `gap_status="Complete Gap"` for 2+ consecutive hours. Route to the Splunk admin team (collection issue) rather than the network team.
• **Root cause triage:** Complete gaps are typically caused by TA input failure, API credential expiration, or Catalyst Center maintenance. Below-baseline hours may be legitimate quiet periods.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Complete gap:** Check TA health: `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ExecProcessor "TA_cisco_catalyst"` for errors. Verify API credentials. Check Catalyst Center availability.
• **Below baseline during holidays/change freezes:** Add holidays to a `catalyst_holidays` lookup and exclude from gap detection.
• **False gap due to time zone mismatch:** Ensure the search head time zone matches the audit log timestamps. Catalyst Center may log in UTC while the search head displays in local time.
grandmaExplanation: We make sure the audit trail is continuous and complete, catching any gaps where logging stopped, which could mean something was tampered with.
